items.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
items.summary: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
items.summary: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: David Hallberg is leading the Australian Ballet into the future with passion and drive
items.summary: The Australian Ballet helped put David Hallberg back together after a grave injury. He now returns that favour and leads our national ballet company with a new verve and energy.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade fire, as US and Iran prepare for peace talks — as it happened
items.summary: Israel says it has planned direct talks with Lebanon, as it continues to trade fire amid the Iran ceasefire.

items.title: NATO chief Mark Rutte defends alliance as Trump threatens US withdrawal over Iran
items.summary: Mark Rutte says European nations and other US allies are playing a vital role in supporting American military operations as he seeks to defend the alliance from Donald Trump's attacks.

items.title: UK deployed military to deter Russian submarines from targeting undersea cables

items.title: Combatting concussion in contact sport

items.title: ADF chief says surveillance data not contributing to war effort but warship could if asked
items.summary: Australia's chief of defence says Australia could "absolutely" deploy a warship to the Strait of Hormuz if asked by the federal government.

items.title: Man charged with murder over Sunshine Coast woman's 'suspicious' kangaroo crash
items.summary: NT police have charged a 33-year-old man with murder over the "suspicious death" of his female partner, who was found dead on an outback road over the weekend, after he alleged she was injured in a crash involving a kangaroo.

items.title: Police investigation underway after officer pushes Indigenous woman to the ground during arrest in Broome
items.summary: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

items.title: Alcoa rejects concerns over increased mercury levels at its Wagerup refinery
items.summary: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

items.title: Bulldogs stun misfiring Panthers in big boilover victory
items.summary: The Penrith Panthers unbeaten start to the NRL season has been ended in comprehensive fashion by the Bulldogs at Stadium Australia.

items.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change

items.title: Crows get home against Blues in the wet in Gather Round opener
items.summary: The Adelaide Crows send the fans home happy after ending its losing streak by beating Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval to start Gather Round. Follow live.

items.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Police calling for witnesses after wanted man left with life-threatening injuries
items.summary: Police allege the 34-year-old "was seen to be acting suspiciously" near homes at Bray Park near Murwillumbah when he was confronted.

items.title: Lance Collard to learn fate over alleged homophobic slur after marathon AFL tribunal hearing
items.summary: Lance Collard may face 10 weeks on the sidelines if found guilty of using a homophobic slur, after an AFL disciplinary tribunal held over its verdict until Friday.

items.title: Iran war fuel crisis financially benefiting Russia and Vladimir Putin

items.title: Matt Canavan's rebrand takes shape in sunglasses, Albanese rebukes Trump
items.summary: With retro shades, big rhetoric and a rejection of net zero, Matt Canavan is hoping to reshape the Nationals. His first big test is just weeks away.

items.title: Jury retires to consider verdict in Irma Palasics murder trial after weeks of evidence
items.summary: Justice David Mossop told the jury it had a "complex" and "substantial" task after the court was presented with 53 witnesses, 14 statements or recordings, and 97 exhibits.

items.title: Gang rapist on the run after escaping custody at Sydney hospital
items.summary: A man convicted of gang raping a 14-year-old girl in a Western Sydney park has escaped from custody while at a hospital. Follow live.

items.title: What makes a relationship de facto and how can it impact a break-up?
items.summary: Our "ordinary understanding" of de facto relationships may not match up with the legal definition and that can cause big surprises for people in the event of a break-up.

items.title: Fremantle convenience store crackdown to curb 'under the counter' illegal nicotine sales
items.summary: The City of Fremantle voted unanimously at Wednesday's council meeting to amend local planning laws, in a bid to stop more convenience stores that sell illicit tobacco products from opening.

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant contributing factor' in Canberra teenager's drowning death, coroner finds

items.title: Bruce Lehrmann's bid to appeal defamation case ruling refused by High Court
items.summary: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that found, on the balance of probabilities, he did rape Brittany Higgins.

items.title: $1 million reward for information on unsolved death of Harmony Bryant

items.title: Four people to face court for alleged offences 'connected' to murder of Adelaide father Chris Robertson

items.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between diesel and food as fuel prices remain high

items.summary: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds has succumbed to injury and will not play against North Queensland.

items.title: Trump's 'end of civilisation' threat could have consequences that outlast the war

items.summary: AFL fans are facing a weekend of wet weather for Gather Round as the South Australian Premier says he is "not going to rush" negotiations on the future of the event.

items.summary: Viki Graham has been remanded in custody after allegedly attacking a man with a hammer at a Sydney hospital overnight.

items.title: Australian playwright S Shakthidharan wins $250,000 Windham-Campbell Prize
items.summary: S Shakthidharan, whose plays include The Wrong Gods, The Jungle and the Sea and Counting and Cracking, has won the prestigious Windham-Campbell Prize.

items.title: Geraldton man charged with murder after death of 69yo man sparks homicide investigation
items.summary: A man dies in hospital after an altercation at a home in Padbury, and police have charged a 39-year-old man who was arrested at the scene with murder.

items.title: China's influence on the US-Iran ceasefire deal

items.title: Fire damages roof of velodrome used at 2016 Rio Olympic Games
items.summary: Rio de Janeiro's Olympic velodrome has been damaged following a fire, which required about 80 firefighters and 20 fire trucks to bring under control.

items.title: NT government in talks with Daly River residents, landowners over potential relocation of Nauiyu
items.summary: Flood-affected residents from Daly River are in talks with the NT government about moving the remote Aboriginal community to higher ground, with a church offering to donate land for the township's relocation.

items.title: More than 1,000 fish killed in Darwin Port after iron ore pollution
items.summary: More than 1,000 small fish die in Darwin Port after acidic run-off from an iron ore stockpile enters a containment pond following heavy rain.

items.title: Melbourne Comedy Festival: Grace Jarvis on comedy as an autistic artform, chronic pain and working in a sex shop
items.summary: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

items.title: Rolling 2026 AFL All-Australian team 1.0: Max Gawn tracking towards league record as Will Ashcroft headlines eight first-timers
items.summary: Melbourne captain Max Gawn shows no sign of slowing down as an excellent first month of the season puts him front and centre of ABC Sport's rolling AFL All-Australian team.

items.title: WA food charities inundated as fuel crisis sends more people to their doors

items.summary: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

items.summary: Daniil Medvedev endures an eventful day at the Monte Carlo Masters, smashing several racquets before suffering the first 6-0, 6-0 trouncing of his distinguished career.

items.title: Darwin's 'dangerous' bus network will soon have officers armed with guns
items.summary: As the conflict in the Middle East continues, more people than ever are turning to public transport to escape the cost of driving — but in Darwin, that choice comes with a different price.

items.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
items.summary: The man's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

items.title: Calls to review Australian food labelling laws as farmers fight cheap imports
items.summary: Farmers battling cheap imported produce want a review of food labelling laws to highlight what Australian produce is included in products.

items.title: As Jess Hull chases elusive international gold, Claudia Hollingsworth is looming over her shoulder
items.summary: Olympic silver medallist Jess Hull is used to setting the benchmark in Australian middle-distance running, but Claudia Hollingsworth is coming for her 1,500-metre crown at this weekend's National Championships.

items.title: Australians paying 'hefty price' for US decisions on Middle East war — as it happened
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says we need the ceasefire to stick and the Strait of Hormuz to be opened for the recovery to begin. Follow live.

items.summary: Homicide squad detectives from Victoria Police are investigating a fatal shooting at a Box Hill apartment building.

items.summary: Perth will be the centre of Italian preseason football in August, with some of the game's heavyweights heading to the Western Australian capital ahead of the next European season.

items.summary: Israeli strikes on Lebanon threaten to scuttle the US-Iran ceasefire as Iranian state media reports the Strait of Hormuz has been closed.

items.summary: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

items.title: Neuroscientist slams AFL's 'potentially dangerous' headgear move
items.summary: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

items.title: Camperdown couple 'relieved' to see home relocated after being built on wrong block
items.summary: A Victorian couple has been forced to move their home that was accidentally built on the wrong block.
